data.title: Cycurion Shareholders Approve Governance Changes and Board Structure
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Cycurion ( (CYCU) ).

On July 23, 2026, Cycurion stockholders approved amendments to the company’s charter and bylaws to establish a classified board of directors divided into three classes with staggered three-year terms, with initial Class I, II and III directors serving until the 2027, 2028 and 2029 annual meetings, respectively. The bylaw changes also centralize control over filling board vacancies, restrict director removal to for-cause actions by a majority of voting power, and introduce advance notice procedures for shareholder director nominations, consolidating governance authority with the current board.

At the 2026 annual meeting, stockholders elected five directors, ratified WWC, P.C. as independent auditor for the year ending December 31, 2026, and approved on an advisory basis both the company’s executive compensation program and the continuation of annual say-on-pay votes. Investors also endorsed an amended and restated equity incentive plan allowing awards on preferred as well as common stock, a broad reverse stock split authorization of up to an aggregate 250-for-1 at the board’s discretion, and an adjournment authority, giving the board expanded flexibility to manage capital structure, compensation, and future shareholder votes.

The most recent analyst rating on (CYCU) stock is a Buy
with a $7.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cycurion stock,
see the CYCU Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CYCU Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CYCU is a Neutral.

CYCU scores low primarily due to weak financial performance (large losses, low margins, and ongoing cash burn). Technical indicators also show sustained bearish momentum with the stock trading well below key moving averages. Corporate events provide some strategic positives (acquisitions and backlog), but are meaningfully offset by Nasdaq delisting risk; valuation is constrained by unprofitability and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on CYCU stock,
click here.

More about Cycurion

Cycurion operates in the corporate governance and technology sector, with a focus on managing capital structure, board oversight and equity-based incentives through its charter and bylaw framework. The company maintains both common and preferred voting stock, uses equity incentive plans to align management and shareholder interests, and relies on an independent registered public accounting firm for financial reporting oversight.

Average Trading Volume: 1,549,807

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $3.29M

data.title: Avalon GloboCare Establishes Flexible $10 Million Equity Facility
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from Avalon GloboCare ( (ALBT) ) is now available.

On July 22, 2026, Change Agents Corporation entered into an Equity Purchase Agreement with Hudson Global Ventures, allowing the company, at its discretion, to sell up to $10 million of common stock over a commitment period of up to 36 months at a base price of $0.30 per share, subject to stock-adjustment provisions and detailed limits on individual drawdowns tied to recent trading prices. The arrangement, which includes a 4.99% ownership cap and Nasdaq-related limitations, is designed to give the company flexible access to equity capital but may dilute existing shareholders, especially as the structure allows variable “put” sizes depending on market performance.

As part of the deal, the company issued a warrant to Hudson Global Ventures to purchase up to 925,925 shares at an exercise price of $0.01 per share, exercisable for five years following stockholder approval, and introduced mechanisms such as a $250,000 buyout right and a potential true-up payment if the share price underperforms before approval. Change Agents also signed a Registration Rights Agreement obligating it to register the resale of shares issued under the equity line and warrant, ensuring the investor can exit its positions while tying the full drawdown of the $10 million facility to the effectiveness and maintenance of the registration statement.

More about Avalon GloboCare

Change Agents Corporation is a publicly traded company listed on Nasdaq, issuing common stock with a par value of $0.0001 per share. The company accesses capital markets through structured equity arrangements and relies on registered offerings and private placements to support its funding needs and ongoing operations.

Average Trading Volume: 1,094,404

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $4.1M

data.title: Nanox Issues Preliminary Q2 2026 Results as Cash Crunch Raises Going-Concern Doubts
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Nano-X Imaging ( (NNOX) ) is now available.

On July 23, 2026, Nanox reported preliminary unaudited results for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, alongside a business update that underscores both commercial traction and financial strain. The company highlighted growing revenue streams from teleradiology, imaging systems and AI solutions, but also disclosed a sharp decline in liquidity and warned that existing cash and deposits likely will not fund operations for 12 months, raising substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern.

Operationally, Nanox signed another U.S. capital equipment deal for its Nanox.ARC system, prepared multiple new ARC and ARC X installations, and completed the first Nanox Imaging Network installation in Philadelphia ahead of initial patient scans and commercial activation. The company also struck a three-year reseller agreement with Vertec Scientific to distribute its Nanox.AI Bone solution in the U.K., reinforcing its AI footprint, while acknowledging that its preliminary figures, including revenue of roughly $4.0–$4.1 million and cash of about $31.4 million, remain subject to change pending completion of its close process and potential impairment assessments.

The most recent analyst rating on (NNOX) stock is a Hold
with a $3.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nano-X Imaging stock,
see the NNOX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NNOX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NNOX is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (large ongoing losses and persistent cash burn) and the earnings-call risks (withdrawn revenue guidance and going-concern warning despite operational progress). Valuation is difficult to support with a negative P/E and no dividend. Technical analysis impact is limited due to missing indicator data, with only elevated volatility (beta 1.57) observable.

To see Spark’s full report on NNOX stock,
click here.

More about Nano-X Imaging

Nanox Imaging Ltd. is an Israel-based medical imaging technology company listed on Nasdaq that focuses on enabling preventive healthcare through an integrated imaging and healthcare services platform. Its ecosystem spans affordable digital X-ray hardware, AI algorithms for CT interpretation, cloud-based software, teleradiology via USARAD, and health IT solutions under Nanox Health IT, aiming to expand access to imaging beyond traditional hospitals and improve early disease detection.

The company’s core products include the Nanox.ARC 3D digital tomosynthesis system, Nanox.AI analytical tools, Nanox.CLOUD for secure image management, and complementary marketplace and IT services designed to streamline workflows and support more efficient care delivery worldwide.

data.title: Nano-X Imaging Files Legal Opinion Backing Remaining $53.9 Million ATM Share Issuance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Nano-X Imaging ( (NNOX) ) has provided an update.

Nano-X Imaging Ltd. filed a Form 6-K on July 23, 2026, to furnish a legal opinion from its Israeli counsel, Meitar Law Offices, on the validity of ordinary shares to be issued under its existing at-the-market equity offering program. The opinion relates to up to $53.9 million of remaining ordinary shares that may be issued from time to time under a Controlled Equity Offering Sales Agreement with Cantor Fitzgerald & Co. and Mizuho Securities USA LLC, supported by an effective shelf registration statement and a newly filed prospectus supplement.

The legal opinion confirms that the prospective ATM shares, once properly authorized and issued under the sales agreement and board approvals, will be validly issued, fully paid and non-assessable. However, counsel notes that Nano-X’s currently authorized share capital may be insufficient to issue the full $53.9 million in shares at current market prices, meaning the company may need shareholder approval to increase its authorized share capital before fully utilizing the program, a factor that could affect the pace and scale of future capital raising under the ATM.

The most recent analyst rating on (NNOX) stock is a Hold

On July 23, 2026, Dynagas LNG Partners LP announced that its board had declared a cash distribution of $0.5625 per unit on its Series A Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Units for the period from May 12, 2026 to August 11, 2026. data.title: Auddia Secures Bridge Financing Amid Pending McCarthy Finney Merger
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Auddia ( (AUUD) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 17, 2026, Auddia issued senior unsecured bridge notes to Thramann Holdings, LT350, Influence Healthcare, and Voyex to provide short-term working capital while its February 17, 2026 merger agreement with McCarthy Finney awaits shareholder approval and satisfaction of closing conditions.

The bridge notes set funding caps for each target company, bear 8.0% annual interest, include conversion and premium repayment features tied to equity financings and change-of-control events, and allow amounts advanced to count toward Auddia’s $12 million net cash requirement, aligning interim financing with the pending merger’s liquidity threshold.

More about Auddia

Auddia Inc., a Delaware-based company led by CEO and Executive Chairman Dr. Jeffrey Thramann, is aligned with a portfolio of early stage, AI-native operating businesses including LT350, Influence Healthcare, and Voyex, all controlled through Thramann Holdings. Following a planned merger with McCarthy Finney Inc., these entities, along with Auddia, are expected to become subsidiaries of a newly public holding company, positioning McCarthy Finney to trade under the ticker symbol MCFN and consolidating AI-focused operations in a single listed vehicle.

On July 17, 2026, Auddia entered into senior unsecured bridge notes with Thramann Holdings, LT350, Influence Healthcare, and Voyex to provide interim funding while a February 17, 2026 merger agreement with McCarthy Finney remains pending and subject to shareholder approval in late August 2026.

The notes authorize up to $360,000 for Thramann Holdings, $400,000 for LT350, $590,000 for Influence Healthcare, and $50,000 for Voyex, carry 8.0% annual interest, and are structured as senior unsecured obligations that restrict each target company from incurring debt senior or secured to these instruments.

For LT350, Influence, and Voyex, the bridge notes include conversion features into future equity rounds at a 20% discount if qualified financings are completed before maturity, while change-of-control events for these companies would trigger a repayment premium equal to 50% of outstanding principal on the respective note.

Importantly for closing the merger, the parties agreed that any funds Auddia advances under these bridge notes will be credited toward Auddia’s $12 million net cash requirement at closing, effectively supporting the liquidity condition for the transaction while supplying working capital to the AI-native targets.

Average Trading Volume: 3,950,574

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $6.54M

data.title: Intelligent Bio Solutions Advances FDA 510(k) Cybersecurity Readiness
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Intelligent Bio Solutions ( (INBS) ) has shared an update.

On July 23, 2026, Intelligent Bio Solutions announced it had resolved all cybersecurity and software-related review items tied to independent penetration testing for its planned FDA 510(k) submission, completing remediation of all medium-risk vulnerabilities and most low-risk issues while fully addressing all software bill of materials vulnerabilities. These measures, independently verified and aligned with FDA guidance, are described by management as materially reducing regulatory risk and strengthening the company’s 510(k) package ahead of a planned filing in the second half of 2026.

The 510(k) submission will initially target codeine detection and is intended to expand the Intelligent Fingerprinting Drug Screening System’s use in the U.S. beyond forensic-only settings into the multi-billion-dollar drug screening market, spanning workplace testing, criminal justice, pain management, law enforcement, and substance abuse treatment. Coupled with a manufacturing partnership expected to deliver over 40% annual production cost savings and roughly 20 percentage points of gross margin improvement, the progress underscores INBS’s preparations for a scalable U.S. launch once regulatory clearance is obtained.

Spark’s Take on INBS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, INBS is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by heavy ongoing losses and cash burn, alongside bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting factors include modest balance-sheet leverage and positive corporate updates advancing FDA 510(k) study work, while valuation is difficult to assess due to negative earnings and no dividend data provided.

To see Spark’s full report on INBS stock,
click here.

More about Intelligent Bio Solutions

Intelligent Bio Solutions Inc. is a medical technology company focused on intelligent, rapid, non-invasive testing solutions, led by its Intelligent Fingerprinting Drug Screening System, which analyzes fingerprint sweat to detect recent drug use. The system, already deployed outside the U.S. across workplace, law enforcement, government, and safety-critical industries, targets substances such as opiates, cocaine, methamphetamine, and cannabis, and is positioned for broader applications.

data.title: InspireMD Receives Nasdaq Notice on Bid Price Compliance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

InspireMD ( (NSPR) ) has shared an announcement.

On July 17, 2026, InspireMD, Inc. disclosed that it received a notice from Nasdaq stating its shares no longer meet the minimum $1.00 bid price requirement, after trading below that threshold for 30 consecutive business days ending July 16, 2026. The company’s stock will continue trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market, but InspireMD faces heightened scrutiny over its share price performance and potential listing status.

Nasdaq has granted InspireMD a 180-day grace period, until January 13, 2027, to restore compliance by sustaining a closing bid price of at least $1.00 for at least ten consecutive business days, with the possibility of an additional compliance period if other listing standards are met. The company plans to monitor its stock and consider measures such as a reverse stock split, highlighting the risk of eventual delisting and increased uncertainty for shareholders if compliance is not regained.

The most recent analyst rating on (NSPR) stock is a Buy
with a $4.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on InspireMD stock,
see the NSPR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NSPR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NSPR is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by heavy ongoing losses and significant cash burn, which increase financing and execution risk. Technicals also remain weak with a broad downtrend despite oversold signals. These are partially offset by strong recent revenue growth and encouraging clinical/regulatory progress, though the U.S. pause/recall and withdrawn guidance temper near-term confidence.

To see Spark’s full report on NSPR stock,

More about InspireMD

InspireMD, Inc. operates in the medical technology sector and is listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker NSPR. The latest announcement is out from Arq Inc ( (ARQ) ).

On July 23, 2026, Arq, Inc. amended the employment agreement of President and CEO Robert Rasmus, setting a new term through July 23, 2029, while reducing his annual salary to $50,000 and eliminating his eligibility for annual bonuses and long-term incentive plans, offset by reimbursement of certain business expenses. In connection with the amendment, the board’s compensation committee granted him 600,000 time-based RSUs and 600,000 performance-based RSUs under the 2026 Omnibus Incentive Plan, with vesting tied to tenure, stock-price milestones up to $9.00, and change-in-control or termination protections, signaling a greater alignment of his compensation with shareholder value.

On July 17, 2026, Arq and Rasmus also revised a prior inducement RSU award covering 400,000 units granted at his 2023 hire, extending the performance period for earning those shares from July 17, 2026 to July 17, 2029. The revised mix of low cash pay, extended equity horizons, and performance-based vesting is likely to reduce near-term cash outflows while incentivizing long-term stock performance, affecting how investors and other stakeholders view executive incentives and governance at the company.

First Western Financial ( (MYFW) ) has issued an announcement.

First Western Financial reported second-quarter 2026 net income available to common shareholders of $5.7 million, or $0.57 per diluted share, down from $6.2 million in the first quarter of 2026 but more than double the $2.5 million earned in the second quarter of 2025. Net interest income rose 4.3% sequentially to $21.8 million, net interest margin improved to 2.90%, and average noninterest-bearing deposits increased 5.1% quarter over quarter, supporting higher book and tangible book value per share.

Management highlighted that second-quarter 2026 performance benefited from disciplined expense control, stable asset quality, and an improved mix of interest-earning assets and deposits, which lowered funding costs and boosted margins. While non-interest income declined due to weaker mortgage loan gains and lower risk management and insurance fees, revenue and profitability remained significantly higher than a year earlier, positioning the bank to leverage strong loan and deposit pipelines and sustain attractive returns for shareholders through 2026.

The company plans to discuss its second-quarter 2026 results with analysts and investors on July 24, 2026, via an investor call and webcast, providing further detail on financial performance and strategic trends. This communication underscores management’s emphasis on balance sheet optimization and relationship-based banking as key drivers of ongoing growth in a competitive regional banking environment.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation Class A ( (FORL) ).

On June 22, 2026, Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation held a special meeting at which shareholders approved amendments to its charter and trust agreement, allowing the board to extend the deadline to complete an initial business combination up to twelve times, in one‑month increments, from June 22, 2026 to June 22, 2027 by funding $75,000 into the trust account for each extension. Shareholders also voted to remove the prior limitation that restricted redemptions based on net tangible asset levels and authorized meeting adjournments if needed, moves that provide the SPAC with greater flexibility to pursue a deal while significantly shrinking its public float after holders of 893,090 Class A shares, about 88 percent of the public shares, chose to redeem, leaving 121,427 Class A shares outstanding.

More about Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation Class A

Four Leaf Acquisition Corporation is a special purpose acquisition company focused on identifying and consummating an initial business combination with an operating business. The company raised capital through its March 22, 2023 initial public offering, with proceeds placed in a trust account managed by Continental Stock Transfer & Trust Company to fund a future transaction and potential shareholder redemptions.

Columbia Banking System ( (COLB) ) has issued an update.

On July 23, 2026, Columbia Banking System, Inc. reported second-quarter 2026 results, posting net income of $208 million and operating net income of $217 million, with diluted earnings per share of $0.73 and operating diluted EPS of $0.76. Management highlighted that commercial loan growth, disciplined execution and ongoing balance sheet repositioning underpinned performance in a dynamic operating environment.

Quarterly net interest income slipped to $589 million and net interest margin edged down to 3.93%, partly due to interest income reversals, while non-interest income rose to $88 million and non-interest expense fell to $375 million as merger costs declined and acquisition-related savings materialized. Credit indicators remained contained, with annualized net charge-offs at 0.25% of average loans, provision expense of $27 million and non-performing assets at 0.42% of total assets, alongside strong capital ratios, a $0.37 per-share dividend paid on June 15, 2026, $199 million of stock repurchases and deposit inflows of about $1.25 billion from 2026 small business and retail deposit campaigns.

Columbia also reported improved operating efficiency, with its efficiency ratio down to 55.15% and operating efficiency ratio at 52.92%, as pre-provision net revenue and operating pre-provision net revenue increased to $302 million and $314 million, respectively. Returns on average assets and common equity rose versus the prior quarter, reinforcing management’s message that the bank’s diversified model, sound credit culture and capital generation are supporting sustainable shareholder value despite modest balance sheet deleveraging.

OBOOK Holdings, Inc. Class A ( (OWLS) ) has shared an update.

In a July 23, 2026 update, OwlTing Group reported that payment volume on OwlPay Harbor, its digital currency-based cross-border enterprise payments platform, grew more than fivefold quarter over quarter in Q2 2026. The company said monthly volume has increased every month since early 2026 and that July’s first three weeks had already surpassed June’s full-month volume.

Across the broader OwlPay platform, spanning fiat and digital currency transactions, cumulative processed payment volume exceeded US$600 million as of June 30, 2026, with 67 enterprise clients onboarded by that date. The business is seeing particular momentum in Asia-focused trade corridors and in regions such as the Middle East and Africa where U.S. dollar access is constrained, suggesting OwlTing’s regulated infrastructure is strengthening its competitive position in cross-border B2B payments and reinforcing its growth trajectory.

To see Spark’s full report on OWLS stock,

More about OBOOK Holdings, Inc. Class A

OwlTing Group, the operating brand of OBOOK Holdings Inc., is a global fintech company focused on building regulated cross-border payment infrastructure, blending fiat and digital currency rails. Its OwlPay platform serves enterprises across the U.S., Asia, Europe and other regions, leveraging licenses in 42 U.S. states, a VASP registration in Poland and a Bank API license in Japan to move funds into harder-to-reach markets.

Founded in Taiwan, the company runs subsidiaries in markets including the United States, Japan, Poland, Singapore, Hong Kong, Thailand and Malaysia, targeting business clients that need faster, cheaper international payments. OwlTing has been recognized among top global digital currency enterprise players and was ranked No. 226 on the Financial Times–Statista High-Growth Companies Asia-Pacific 2026 list, reflecting a 42% compound annual growth rate.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A ( (ZBAO) ) is now available.

On July 23, 2026, Zhibao Technology Inc. reported that it has elected to follow certain Cayman Islands home country corporate governance practices instead of specified Nasdaq rules, including those requiring a majority-independent board, regular meetings of independent directors, annual shareholder meetings, and shareholder approvals for major equity issuances and compensation plans. The company disclosed that its Cayman counsel, Ogier (Cayman) LLP, confirmed these practices are permissible under Cayman law and the firm’s governing documents, signaling a governance framework that departs in several respects from typical U.S. domestic standards while otherwise remaining broadly aligned with Nasdaq requirements, with implications for how shareholder rights and board oversight are structured.

Spark’s Take on ZBAO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ZBAO is a Underperform.

ZBAO scores low primarily due to weak financial performance: a sharp revenue decline, a return to significant losses, and persistent negative operating/free cash flow that raises funding and balance-sheet resilience risk. Technical indicators reinforce the caution, with the stock trading well below key moving averages and negative momentum signals. Valuation is also not a clear positive, as the negative P/E reflects current losses and no dividend yield is available to offset risk.

To see Spark’s full report on ZBAO stock,
click here.

More about Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A

Zhibao Technology Inc., a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market, operates as a foreign private issuer subject to Nasdaq’s corporate governance listing standards. The company follows corporate governance practices permitted under Cayman Islands law and its memorandum and articles of association, while selectively using exemptions available to non-U.S. issuers.
